# LiteOn Technology — Power Supplies + Thermal Components
Last updated: 2026-04-01

## Company Overview
- **HQ:** Taipei, Taiwan
- Listed on Taiwan Stock Exchange (2301.TW)
- Revenue: ~$5B USD annually
- Divisions: LiteOn Power (PSU), LiteOn Semiconductor, LiteOn Cloud Infrastructure
- Major OEM supplier: HP, Dell, Lenovo, Supermicro, Foxconn

## Role in NVL72 Supply Chain
- LiteOn is primarily a **power supply** supplier for NVL72 trays — not the liquid cooling manifold itself
- They supply the high-efficiency PSUs and DC/DC power conversion modules inside compute trays
- LiteOn Power: Titanium-rated (96%+) server PSUs — present in virtually all major server platforms
- If listed as a "tray inner" supplier: LiteOn is the PSU/power module layer adjacent to (not part of) the liquid cooling manifold

## Products Relevant to NVL72
- Server PSUs: 3kW, 6kW, 10kW+ high-power units for GPU servers
- DC/DC converter modules (48V intermediate bus)
- ORV3-compliant power shelf units
- Cooling: LiteOn also makes thermal modules and has expanded into liquid cooling components (cold plate assemblies, manifold sub-assemblies) — this is the growing segment

## Liquid Cooling Expansion
- LiteOn has invested in liquid cooling manufacturing post-2024
- Cold plate assemblies for GPU servers: expanding product line
- Potential manifold sub-assembly capability — details not fully public

## ADC Relevance
- LiteOn PSU = inside every major GPU server tray, including NVL72
- PSU lead time = independent constraint from manifold lead time
- Not a direct ADC vendor for liquid cooling (yet)

## URLs
- liteon.com
- liteon.com/en-us/Products/Power/Server-Products/

## Confidence: HIGH for PSU role; MEDIUM for liquid cooling manifold role (expansion confirmed, NVL72 specific role not fully public)
